compiled_state.description:
# BUG: MCP run_workflow bypasses REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM for v3 branch/loop/map LLM steps

## Reproduction Steps
1. On a write-enabled MCP server with `REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM` unset, call `run_workflow("plan-review", <ticket>)`.
2. Observe the ALLOW_LLM fence (`mcp_server.py:946`) is skipped and billable single-turn finder calls fire.

Root cause: `has_llm_steps._scan` (`src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py:285-308`) flattens a v3 branch
step's `{when, then:[...], else:[...]}` dict via `list(values())`, so the bare-array arms become
list items that are skipped by `if not isinstance(s, dict): continue`. Empirically `has_llm_steps`
returns **False** for both `gates/plan-review.yaml` and `gates/completion-verification.yaml` (their
LLM steps live only inside branch arms), while `code-review.yaml` (top-level LLM steps) returns True.

Expected: `has_llm_steps` returns True for any workflow with an LLM step nested in a v3 branch/loop/map arm.
Actual: returns False -> the documented billing/policy gate is bypassed on default-shipped gates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_scan` descends into bare-array arms (v3 `then`/`else`/`body`) after flattening.
- [ ] Regression test asserts True for a minimal v3 branch-with-LLM-arm AND for the shipped
      `plan-review.yaml` + `completion-verification.yaml` gates.
- [ ] `make lint` + the workflow test suite pass; commit carries a `rebar-ticket:` trailer.
compiled_state.title: BUG: run_workflow bypasses REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM for v3 branch/loop/map LLM steps
